{"key_event":"Independence crisis and French withdrawal","reasoning":"Civil society became more powerful through wartime associational growth, parties, unions, press activism, and especially the 1943 mass mobilization that forced release of Lebanese leaders, though politics remained mediated by confessional elites and patronage. State power rose with independence, Lebanonization of administration, creation of the Lebanese Army, international recognition, and French troop withdrawal, but remained only moderate because capacity was constrained by sectarian bargaining, weak revenue/administrative reach, and manipulated patronage politics.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":6.2,"state_change":0.9,"state_power":6.5}
